channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Thrive Grant 2026: Up to $30,000 for Organizations Advancing Plant-Based Food Systems Worldwide
item.dc:creator: Maria Ramirez
item.description:
Organizations working to build healthier, more sustainable food systems can now apply for the Thrive Grant 2026. This funding opportunity supports impactful initiatives outside the United States that promote plant-based foods, improve access to nutritious meals, and accelerate alternatives to industrial animal agriculture. Eligible organizations can receive between $10,000 and $30,000 USD to scale projects […]

The post Thrive Grant 2026: Up to $30,000 for Organizations Advancing Plant-Based Food Systems Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Organizations working to build healthier, more sustainable food systems can now apply for the Thrive Grant 2026. This funding opportunity supports impactful initiatives outside the United States that promote plant-based foods, improve access to nutritious meals, and accelerate alternatives to industrial animal agriculture.

Eligible organizations can receive between $10,000 and $30,000 USD to scale projects that create lasting change in communities around the world.

What is the Thrive Grant?

The Thrive Grant is an international funding program designed for organizations with a proven track record of success. It supports mission-driven groups advancing food system transformation through innovation, education, advocacy, and community-based solutions.

The grant is especially suited for mid-size or larger organizations ready to expand their reach and deepen their impact.

Grant Value

Successful applicants may receive:

  • Minimum Funding: $10,000 USD
  • Maximum Funding: $30,000 USD

Funding amounts depend on the quality, feasibility, and impact potential of the proposal.

Application Deadlines

The Thrive Grant operates on multiple funding rounds each year. Applications close at 11:59 PM GMT on the following dates:

  • 1 June 2026 (Next Upcoming Deadline)
  • 1 September 2026
  • 1 December 2026
  • 1 March 2027

Applicants can choose the nearest available round that best fits their project timeline.

Response Time

Applicants should expect a response within up to 8 weeks after the application deadline.

Who Can Apply?

Organizations may be eligible if they meet the following requirements:

  • Based outside the United States
  • Not an international chapter of a US-based organization
  • Nonprofit or mission-driven organization
  • Strong reputation within their community and stakeholder network
  • Clear track record of successful projects
  • Proposal aligns with plant-based food systems or alternatives to industrial animal agriculture
  • Meets Thrive Philanthropy’s safespace policy

Currently, organizations with annual budgets under $500,000 USD are being prioritized.

Priority Areas for Funding

Thrive Grant welcomes proposals focused on the following areas:

1. Alternatives to Industrial Animal Agriculture

Projects that reduce dependence on factory farming and introduce sustainable solutions.

2. Plant-Rich Food Systems

Initiatives that encourage healthier food environments through plant-based options.

3. Access to Nutritious Plant-Based Foods

Programs that improve affordability, availability, and awareness of healthy plant-based nutrition.

Special Note for Africa-Based Applicants

Due to a high number of proposals from Africa, first-time Africa-based applicants who have not previously received Thrive funding are encouraged to join the Thrive Africa Accelerator before applying.

The accelerator helps organizations connect with advocates across Africa while allowing Thrive to better understand their work.

Additionally, first-time Africa-based groups may have stronger chances through the Seed Grant pathway.

Ineligible Projects

The following are not eligible for Thrive Grant funding:

  • For-profit companies or businesses
  • Restaurants or commercial ventures
  • Animal farming or animal-use projects
  • Projects using animal-based products
  • Cellular agriculture initiatives
  • Food giveaways or hunger-relief distributions
  • Farmed animal welfare campaigns
  • Tuition, student research, or university fees
  • Political parties or candidate endorsements
  • Military or police food aid programs
  • Work inside the United States
  • Chapters of large international organizations

Why You Should Apply

The Thrive Grant is more than financial support—it is an opportunity to grow solutions that improve public health, sustainability, and food equity. If your organization is building a better future through plant-based innovation, this grant can help you expand your impact.

How to Apply

Applications must be submitted through the official online portal. Email submissions are not accepted.

APPLY HERE. 

For more information about this apportunity, click here. 

Discover more grant opportunities, click here. 

The post Thrive Grant 2026: Up to $30,000 for Organizations Advancing Plant-Based Food Systems Worldwide appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Wattle Fellowship 2026 by The University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Sustainability Leadership Program for University Students (Up to $2,000 Project Funding + Mentorship + Retreats)
item.dc:creator: Mercy N. Atsuki
item.description:
Are you passionate about sustainability and ready to grow your leadership impact? The Wattle Fellowship by the University of Melbourne 2026 offers a unique, fully funded opportunity for students to develop their leadership skills while working on real-world sustainability challenges. Whether you come from arts, science, engineering, law, or any other field, this program is […]

The post Wattle Fellowship 2026 by The University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Sustainability Leadership Program for University Students (Up to $2,000 Project Funding + Mentorship + Retreats) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you passionate about sustainability and ready to grow your leadership impact? The Wattle Fellowship by the University of Melbourne 2026 offers a unique, fully funded opportunity for students to develop their leadership skills while working on real-world sustainability challenges. Whether you come from arts, science, engineering, law, or any other field, this program is designed to nurture diverse thinkers and changemakers.

Applications are open until 24 May 2026, and students from both the University of Melbourne and La Trobe University are encouraged to apply.

Why the Wattle Fellowship Matters

Leadership in sustainability is no longer limited to one discipline. The Wattle Fellowship recognizes that impactful change comes from a blend of perspectives—artists, engineers, policy thinkers, and grassroots advocates all have a role to play.

This program creates a space where students can:

  • Build confidence in their leadership abilities
  • Develop practical solutions to sustainability issues
  • Join a network of like-minded changemakers

Even if you feel you don’t meet every requirement, you are still encouraged to apply. The program values authenticity, growth potential, and purpose over perfection.

What You’ll Get

The Wattle Fellowship provides a comprehensive support system to help you grow personally and professionally:

1. Fully Funded Development Opportunities

  • Access to workshops, retreats, and mentoring programs
  • No participation costs—everything is fully funded

2. Project Funding

  • Up to $2,000 to implement your own sustainability Action Project
  • Practical experience turning ideas into impact

3. Personalized Coaching

  • Guidance to create a Leadership Development Plan
  • Support in designing and executing your sustainability project

4. Academic Recognition

  • Official recognition on your academic transcript

5. Lifelong Network

  • Become part of a strong, ongoing community of changemakers

6. Additional Support

  • Equity Grants for students facing financial barriers
  • Travel support for students based in regional areas

Who Can Apply?

To be eligible, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be a current student (undergraduate, postgraduate, or PhD) at:
    • University of Melbourne
    • La Trobe University
  • Be in good academic standing (no minimum WAM required)
  • Be enrolled at the start of the fellowship (you may graduate during the program)
  • Be either:
    • Domestic or international student
    • Part-time or full-time student

Important Note

  • Students currently overseas can apply, but must be in Victoria, Australia when the program begins

Read Before You Apply

To help you prepare a strong application and avoid common misunderstandings, take note of the following key points:

  • The application is designed to take approximately 1.5–2 hours, with a focus on honest reflection rather than perfectly crafted answers.
  • Students from both the University of Melbourne and La Trobe University are fully eligible to apply for the 2026 cohort.
  • If you are currently overseas, you can still apply. The written stage is conducted online, and accommodations may be made for selection stages. However, you must be physically present in Victoria, Australia when the program begins.
  • Applicants must be able to actively participate in in-person workshops and retreats. Short absences may be acceptable, but extended time away from Victoria during the fellowship is not permitted.
  • This opportunity is strictly for current students. If you have already graduated, you are not eligible to apply.
  • If you are scheduled to graduate during the fellowship, you may still participate, provided you remain engaged and meet all program commitments.
  • There is no minimum academic score requirement. However, you must be in good academic standing at your university.
  • International students are welcome and encouraged to apply.
  • Students studying at regional campuses may receive additional financial support to cover travel costs for attending program activities in Melbourne.

How to Apply

The application process is designed to evaluate both your thinking and your authenticity:

Step 1: Written Application

  • Respond to a set of reflective questions
  • Expected completion time: 1.5–2 hours

Step 2: Shortlisting

  • Selected applicants are invited to the next stage

Step 3: Selection Day

  • Participate in:
    • Individual interviews
    • Group activities

Optional Submission

  • You may include:
    • A short video
    • A portfolio
    • Any work that represents who you are
  • Apply HERE

What the Selection Team Looks For

The Wattle Fellowship is committed to diversity and does not follow a rigid selection formula. However, successful applicants often demonstrate the following:

1. Commitment to Sustainability

  • A genuine interest in sustainability issues
  • Evidence of care through actions or ideas

2. Connection to Purpose

  • A clear (or developing) sense of personal motivation
  • Understanding of why sustainability matters to you

3. Track Record of Action

  • Participation in initiatives such as:
    • Student clubs
    • Community projects
    • Research
    • Personal initiatives

4. Reflectiveness

  • Ability to acknowledge growth areas
  • Willingness to learn from both successes and failures

5. Coachability

  • Openness to feedback and challenges
  • Willingness to grow through discomfort

6. Collaboration and Cohort Fit

  • Supportive attitude toward peers
  • Ability to uplift others’ ideas and growth

For more information about this opportunity, click HERE

Application Deadline

  • Deadline: 24 May 2026

Discover more fellowship opportunities on OFY HERE

The post Wattle Fellowship 2026 by The University of Melbourne: Fully Funded Sustainability Leadership Program for University Students (Up to $2,000 Project Funding + Mentorship + Retreats) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Innovate UK Frontier AI Funding 2026: Up to £50,000 for Feasibility Studies and a Pathway to Major AI Investment
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Artificial intelligence startups, researchers, and innovation-driven organizations in the United Kingdom now have a new opportunity to access funding through Innovate UK’s Frontier AI Discovery programme. This initiative is designed to support ambitious projects in frontier artificial intelligence, machine learning, and foundation models through early-stage feasibility grants. It also serves as the first phase of […]

The post Innovate UK Frontier AI Funding 2026: Up to £50,000 for Feasibility Studies and a Pathway to Major AI Investment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Artificial intelligence startups, researchers, and innovation-driven organizations in the United Kingdom now have a new opportunity to access funding through Innovate UK’s Frontier AI Discovery programme.

This initiative is designed to support ambitious projects in frontier artificial intelligence, machine learning, and foundation models through early-stage feasibility grants. It also serves as the first phase of a larger funding pipeline that could lead to substantial collaborative research and development support in a future second phase.

For organizations working on next-generation AI technologies, this programme offers a valuable opportunity to validate ideas, build partnerships, and prepare for large-scale implementation.

What Is the Frontier AI Discovery Programme?

The Frontier AI Discovery programme is an investment by Innovate UK to accelerate the development of transformative AI technologies.

Phase 1 focuses on feasibility studies that help organizations assess technical viability, validate business models, and form strong consortia for future development.

Selected applicants may later be invited into Phase 2, where larger collaborative projects could receive significant R&D support.

Funding Available

The programme offers the following financial support:

  • Phase 1 feasibility grants: £25,000 to £50,000 per project
  • Total Phase 1 funding pot: £2.5 million
  • Future Phase 2 funding pipeline: at least £50 million for demonstrator projects

What the Programme Funds

Applicants can seek funding for projects involving:

  • Feasibility studies for frontier AI and foundation models
  • New AI and machine learning capabilities
  • Consortium building for future R&D collaboration
  • Business model testing
  • Technical de-risking before larger deployment

Priority Mission Areas

Projects should align with one or more strategic missions:

  • AI-enabled health and life sciences
  • Advanced materials powered by AI
  • Secure AI for defence and national security
  • Fundamental AI and foundation model development

Who Can Apply

The opportunity is open to:

  • UK-registered organizations
  • AI startups and SMEs
  • Large companies
  • Research institutions
  • Teams preparing collaborative AI research consortia

Applicants should be actively developing frontier AI technologies or advanced machine learning systems with long-term commercial or societal value.

Future Phase 2 Opportunity

Applicants that progress may gain access to a larger second phase that includes:

  • Project costs between £5 million and £10 million
  • Project duration of 24 to 32 months
  • Completion by March 31, 2030
  • Collaboration between industry, SMEs, and academia

This creates a long-term pathway from concept validation to full-scale innovation delivery.

Application Deadline

The application deadline is June 10, 2026, at 11:00 AM UK time.

Early submission is recommended to avoid technical issues and allow time for proposal refinement.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can submit their proposals through the official application portal.

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Innovate UK Frontier AI Funding 2026: Up to £50,000 for Feasibility Studies and a Pathway to Major AI Investment appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: a16z Speedrun Accelerator: Get Up to $1M in Funding and Scale Your Startup Globally
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The a16z Speedrun Accelerator 2026 offers early-stage founders a rare opportunity to access up to $1 million in funding, world-class mentorship, and a global network to rapidly scale their startups. Designed for ambitious entrepreneurs worldwide, this 12-week accelerator by Andreessen Horowitz provides the capital, tools, and expert support needed to build high-growth, venture-backed companies. About […]

The post a16z Speedrun Accelerator: Get Up to $1M in Funding and Scale Your Startup Globally appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The a16z Speedrun Accelerator 2026 offers early-stage founders a rare opportunity to access up to $1 million in funding, world-class mentorship, and a global network to rapidly scale their startups. Designed for ambitious entrepreneurs worldwide, this 12-week accelerator by Andreessen Horowitz provides the capital, tools, and expert support needed to build high-growth, venture-backed companies.

About the Program

The a16z Speedrun Accelerator is a highly competitive startup program run by Andreessen Horowitz (a16z), one of the world’s leading venture capital firms. The program is specifically designed for early-stage founders who are building innovative, scalable businesses across sectors.

Selected startups receive up to $500,000 in upfront investment, along with an additional $500,000 reserved for future funding rounds. Beyond capital, participants gain access to over $5 million in credits from leading technology providers, enabling them to build and scale their products efficiently.

The accelerator runs for 12 weeks in San Francisco, bringing together a select cohort of founders from around the world. During this period, participants work closely with experienced operators, investors, and industry experts who provide hands-on support across key areas such as product development, marketing, hiring, and fundraising.

Why This Opportunity Matters

For many early-stage founders, especially those outside major startup hubs, access to capital, networks, and mentorship can be a significant barrier to growth. The a16z Speedrun Accelerator addresses this gap by offering not just funding, but a comprehensive ecosystem designed to accelerate startup success.

The program’s global accessibility is particularly important. Founders from any country can apply, and selected participants receive visa support to relocate temporarily to the United States. This opens doors for entrepreneurs who may otherwise lack access to top-tier startup ecosystems.

Additionally, the program’s selective nature—accepting less than 0.4% of applicants—ensures that participants are surrounded by a high-caliber peer network. This creates an environment where founders can learn from each other, receive valuable feedback, and build long-term collaborations.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the a16z Speedrun Accelerator benefit from a powerful combination of financial support, operational expertise, and strategic connections. The program provides immediate capital to help founders move quickly, experiment, and refine their business models without the constraints of limited resources.

Founders receive hands-on guidance from experienced operators across multiple domains, including marketing strategy, go-to-market execution, hiring, and business development. This level of support helps startups avoid common pitfalls and accelerate their growth trajectory.

In addition to mentorship, participants gain access to a16z’s extensive network, which includes executives and decision-makers from over 2,000 companies. This network can open doors to partnerships, customers, and future investors.

The program also prepares founders for fundraising through structured support, including pitch development, practice sessions, and a high-profile Demo Day attended by over 1,000 early-stage investors. This significantly increases the chances of securing additional investment.

Who Can Apply

The accelerator is open to early-stage founders from anywhere in the world who are building scalable startups. There are no strict geographic restrictions, making it accessible to entrepreneurs from emerging markets as well as established startup ecosystems.

Applicants should demonstrate strong technical or business capabilities, a clear vision for their startup, and the potential to build a high-growth company. While the program is highly competitive, it is open to founders at various stages of development, including those who are just beginning to build their products.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is global, with applications open to founders from all countries. Selected participants will attend the program in San Francisco, with visa support provided to facilitate their participation.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested founders must submit an online application detailing their startup, team, and vision. Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, but all submissions must be completed before the deadline.

The application deadline is May 17, 2026, at 11:59 PM PT. The selected cohort will run from July 27 to October 11, 2026.

Given the program’s highly selective nature, applicants are encouraged to clearly articulate their value proposition, market opportunity, and growth potential in their submissions.

Conclusion

The a16z Speedrun Accelerator 2026 stands out as one of the most competitive and rewarding opportunities for early-stage founders globally. By combining significant funding, expert mentorship, and access to a powerful network, the program equips entrepreneurs with the resources they need to build impactful, scalable companies.

For founders ready to take their startup to the next level, this accelerator offers a gateway into the global venture ecosystem and the chance to accelerate growth at an unprecedented pace.

Learn more and apply on the a16z speedrun website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post a16z Speedrun Accelerator: Get Up to $1M in Funding and Scale Your Startup Globally appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: African Youth Negotiators Fellowship 2026: One of Africa’s Most Competitive Climate Leadership Fellowships
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is one of the most prestigious and transformative opportunities currently open to young people across Africa who are passionate about climate change, diplomacy, and global policy engagement. If you are a young African leader seeking to influence international climate negotiations and shape the continent’s future, this fellowship provides […]

The post African Youth Negotiators Fellowship 2026: One of Africa’s Most Competitive Climate Leadership Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) 2026 is one of the most prestigious and transformative opportunities currently open to young people across Africa who are passionate about climate change, diplomacy, and global policy engagement. If you are a young African leader seeking to influence international climate negotiations and shape the continent’s future, this fellowship provides a powerful platform to build your capacity, network, and impact.

Overview of the African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF)

The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship (AYNF) is a pan-African leadership and capacity-building program designed to empower young Africans with the skills required to actively participate in global climate governance, especially within frameworks such as the United Nations climate negotiation processes.

This initiative was developed to address a critical gap: while Africa contributes less than 4% of global emissions, it suffers disproportionately from climate impacts. The fellowship ensures that African youth are not merely observers but active contributors in shaping climate policy and negotiations.

The program is organized by the Green Africa Youth Organization (GAYO) in collaboration with institutions such as the Environmental Protection Authority (EPA) Ghana and the Climate and Development Knowledge Network (CDKN).

Why This Fellowship Matters

The AYNF is not just another youth program—it is a strategic pipeline for developing Africa’s next generation of climate negotiators. Participants gain:

  • Real-world negotiation skills
  • Policy writing and analysis expertise
  • Exposure to international climate frameworks like UNFCCC
  • Mentorship from experienced climate leaders
  • Opportunities to participate in global climate conferences such as COP

The program bridges the gap between climate knowledge and actionable policy influence, making it one of the most impactful fellowships available to African youth today.

Structure of the Fellowship Program

The AYNF 2026 fellowship is delivered in three intensive phases, each designed to progressively build participants’ competencies:

Phase 1: Foundational Climate Learning

Participants must complete an online course (Climate Science 101) via the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal and submit a certificate.

This stage ensures that all applicants have a strong understanding of climate science fundamentals before advancing.

Phase 2: Hybrid Intensive Training in Ghana

Selected fellows participate in a one-week hybrid training program featuring:

  • Climate negotiation simulations
  • Policy drafting exercises
  • Deep dives into UNFCCC frameworks
  • Regional climate policy discussions

Phase 3: Mentorship and Global Engagement

The final phase includes:

  • Mentorship programs
  • Negotiation labs
  • Policy brief development
  • Participation in pre-, during-, and post-COP (Conference of Parties) activities

This phase ensures fellows are fully prepared to engage at national, regional, and global levels.

Who Can Apply? (Eligibility Criteria)

The fellowship is specifically designed for young Africans with a passion for climate leadership.

Eligible Applicants Must:

  • Be citizens of an African country
  • Be aged 15 to 35 years
  • Demonstrate strong interest or experience in climate policy, advocacy, or diplomacy
  • Be proficient in English (the program language)
  • Complete at least one course on the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal
  • Be able to fully commit to all three phases of the program

Target Audience (Who This Opportunity Is For)

This fellowship is ideal for:

  • Students and recent graduates in environmental studies, political science, law, or related fields
  • Youth activists and climate advocates
  • Policy enthusiasts and aspiring diplomats
  • NGO workers focused on sustainability or climate action
  • Young professionals seeking to influence international climate negotiations

Mandatory Requirement Before Applying

Before submitting your application, you must complete the Climate Science 101 course on the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal and upload your certificate.

Failure to do this will automatically disqualify your application.

Benefits of the Fellowship

While highly competitive, the AYNF offers substantial benefits:

1. Advanced Skill Development – Participants gain technical expertise in climate negotiations, policy writing, and diplomacy.

2. Networking Opportunities – Fellows connect with:

  • African Group of Negotiators
  • Climate experts
  • Policy leaders
  • Fellow young changemakers across Africa

3. Global Exposure – Participants may engage in international climate events such as COP conferences.

4. Mentorship – Structured mentorship ensures long-term professional development.

5. Accommodation Support – The program covers accommodation and meals during training, though participants cover their own travel costs.

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

Step 1: Complete Climate Science Training

  • Visit the Ghana Climate Literacy Portal
  • Enroll in “Climate Science 101”
  • Complete all modules and assessments
  • Download your certificate

Step 2: Prepare Your Documents

  • Certificate from Climate Science 101
  • Personal details and background information
  • Evidence of interest or experience in climate issues

Step 3: Submit Online Application

Applications must be submitted through the official portal. Email submissions are not accepted.

Apply here

Selection Process

The fellowship is highly competitive, with thousands of applicants each year.

Shortlisted candidates may be required to complete additional tasks such as:

  • Advanced coursework
  • Quizzes or assignments
  • Interviews or assessments

Only successful candidates are contacted.

Key Tips to Strengthen Your Application

To stand out:

  • Demonstrate clear passion for climate justice
  • Highlight any policy, advocacy, or leadership experience
  • Show commitment to Africa’s climate future
  • Ensure your application is complete and accurate
  • Submit well before the deadline

Important Notes

  • The program is conducted entirely in English
  • Only shortlisted applicants will receive feedback
  • Incomplete applications are automatically rejected
  • Travel costs are not covered

Deadline

30th April 2026

The African Youth Negotiators Fellowship 2026 is more than just a fellowship—it is a gateway to global climate leadership. With its rigorous training, mentorship, and international exposure, it equips young Africans to actively shape climate policy and negotiations at the highest levels.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post African Youth Negotiators Fellowship 2026: One of Africa’s Most Competitive Climate Leadership Fellowships appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund 2026: Unlock Funding and Mentorship for Youth-Led Social Enterprises in Ghana
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund 2026 offers a unique opportunity for young social entrepreneurs in Ghana to access funding, mentorship, and business development support to scale their impact-driven ventures. Designed for youth-led social enterprises, this initiative supports early-stage businesses that are tackling social challenges while building sustainable enterprises. About the Program The Youth […]

The post Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund 2026: Unlock Funding and Mentorship for Youth-Led Social Enterprises in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund 2026 offers a unique opportunity for young social entrepreneurs in Ghana to access funding, mentorship, and business development support to scale their impact-driven ventures. Designed for youth-led social enterprises, this initiative supports early-stage businesses that are tackling social challenges while building sustainable enterprises.

About the Program

The Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund is part of a broader advocacy initiative funded by Plan International Netherlands and implemented in Ghana, Uganda, and Jordan. In Ghana, the program focuses on strengthening youth leadership, entrepreneurship, and advocacy by supporting young people aged 15 to 35 who are leading social enterprises.

The fund specifically targets early-stage ventures that demonstrate both social impact and growth potential. Through a structured support system, selected enterprises receive financial assistance of up to €2,000 alongside tailored capacity-building interventions. These include business development training, mentorship, networking opportunities, and technical support designed to help entrepreneurs refine their operations and scale sustainably.

The program is implemented in key regions including Accra Metropolitan, Ho, Hohoe, and Jasikan, with a strong emphasis on inclusivity. Particular attention is given to supporting young women, girls, persons with disabilities, and underrepresented groups.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Access to funding and structured support remains a major barrier for young entrepreneurs, especially those building businesses with a social mission. The YAC Fund addresses this gap by combining financial support with long-term capacity development.

Beyond funding, the initiative plays a critical role in strengthening the broader ecosystem for youth-led enterprises in Ghana. By connecting entrepreneurs to advocacy spaces and policy processes, the program ensures that young people are not only building businesses but also influencing decision-making at local and national levels.

This dual focus on entrepreneurship and advocacy makes the YAC Fund particularly valuable for those who want to drive systemic change while building sustainable ventures.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the YAC Fund benefit from a comprehensive support package designed to accelerate both business growth and social impact. Selected enterprises receive grant funding of up to €2,000, which can be used for operational costs, equipment, or business development needs.

In addition to financial support, entrepreneurs gain access to tailored business development training that strengthens their management skills, operational efficiency, and strategic planning. Mentorship and coaching from experienced professionals provide guidance on refining business models and navigating growth challenges.

The program also facilitates access to strategic networks, including private sector partners, fellow entrepreneurs, and industry experts. These connections can open doors to partnerships, market opportunities, and future funding.

Importantly, participants are supported in formalizing their businesses, improving regulatory compliance, and enhancing their credibility within the market. They also gain opportunities to engage in advocacy and policy discussions, amplifying their voice and impact.

Who Can Apply

The YAC Fund is open to youth-led social enterprises in Ghana. Applicants must be between the ages of 15 and 35 and demonstrate meaningful youth leadership within their organization.

Eligible enterprises should be at an early stage of development but already generating consistent revenue for at least six months. The business must have a clear social impact focus and align with priority sectors such as agribusiness, climate solutions, health services, or the creative industries.

Additionally, applicants must be operating within the designated regions of Accra Metropolitan, Ho, Hohoe, or Jasikan. The program strongly encourages applications from young women, persons with disabilities, and other underrepresented groups.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is specifically available to social enterprises operating in Ghana, particularly within Accra Metropolitan, Ho, Hohoe, and Jasikan. The program prioritizes ventures that are locally rooted and contribute to community development while addressing broader social challenges.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested applicants must submit their applications through an online platform within two weeks of the official call. The application requires key documents such as an organizational profile, budget and work plan, and proof of an active business bank account.

The selection process includes an initial review by a panel of experts, followed by a due diligence phase to assess shortlisted enterprises. Final selections will be made based on business potential, social impact, and alignment with program objectives.

The deadline to apply is 6 May 2026. Selected enterprises will receive support for a period of approximately 6 to 12 months, depending on their stage of growth and specific needs.

Learn more and apply on the official application link.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Youth Accelerator for Change (YAC) Fund 2026: Unlock Funding and Mentorship for Youth-Led Social Enterprises in Ghana appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Young Leaders Program 2026 in Luanda: A Transformative Opportunity for Emerging Professionals
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Young Leaders Program in Luanda, an exciting opportunity designed to equip the next generation of leaders with the skills, networks, and experiences needed to thrive in today’s dynamic world. This program is ideal for final-year students and early-career professionals who are eager to accelerate their personal and professional growth […]

The post Young Leaders Program 2026 in Luanda: A Transformative Opportunity for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Young Leaders Program in Luanda, an exciting opportunity designed to equip the next generation of leaders with the skills, networks, and experiences needed to thrive in today’s dynamic world.

This program is ideal for final-year students and early-career professionals who are eager to accelerate their personal and professional growth while making a meaningful impact.

Why You Should Apply

The Young Leaders Program offers a unique platform to develop leadership capabilities, gain exposure to real-world challenges, and connect with like-minded peers and industry experts.

Participants will benefit from a structured experience that fosters innovation, collaboration, and forward-thinking leadership.

Key Benefits

By joining the program, participants can expect:

  • Access to leadership development and mentorship opportunities
  • Exposure to global perspectives and real-world problem-solving
  • Networking with high-potential peers and professionals
  • Personal and professional growth in a dynamic environment

Who Should Apply

This opportunity is tailored for:

  • Final-year university students
  • Young professionals with up to 2 years of work experience

If you are driven, curious, and ready to take the next step in your leadership journey, this program is for you.

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by May 22, 2026. Late submissions may not be considered, so early application is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Interested candidates can apply directly through the official application link.

For more Fellowship opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Young Leaders Program 2026 in Luanda: A Transformative Opportunity for Emerging Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator 2026: Global Program to Scale Youth-Led Climate Startups
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator 2026 is a global four-month hybrid program designed to support youth-led startups and businesses working in renewable energy, energy efficiency, and climate technology. Led by the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) with support from the Government of the UAE and partners including Enel Foundation and Social Alpha, this accelerator […]

The post IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator 2026: Global Program to Scale Youth-Led Climate Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator 2026 is a global four-month hybrid program designed to support youth-led startups and businesses working in renewable energy, energy efficiency, and climate technology. Led by the International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA) with support from the Government of the UAE and partners including Enel Foundation and Social Alpha, this accelerator provides young entrepreneurs with the tools, mentorship, and networks needed to grow and scale their climate-focused ventures.

About the Program

The NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator was launched to empower young innovators to play a leading role in the global energy transition. Now in its fourth year, the program continues to support startups that are developing solutions to some of the world’s most pressing climate and energy challenges.

The accelerator runs over four months, combining virtual and in-person engagement. During this period, selected participants will work closely with technical and business experts to refine their business models, improve operational strategies, and enhance their readiness for investment. The program is structured to provide both foundational knowledge and practical support, ensuring that startups can move from early-stage ideas to scalable solutions.

Participants will take part in a series of workshops, webinars, and mentoring sessions designed to strengthen their understanding of renewable energy markets and climate technologies. In addition, the program includes opportunities for direct engagement with investors and industry stakeholders, helping startups build connections that can support long-term growth.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As the global community accelerates efforts toward a sustainable future, there is an increasing need for innovative, youth-driven solutions in the energy and climate sectors. However, many early-stage startups face challenges in accessing funding, mentorship, and networks that are critical for growth.

The IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator addresses these gaps by creating a platform where young entrepreneurs can develop their ideas with guidance from global experts. By supporting startups in renewable energy and climate technology, the program contributes to broader efforts to reduce emissions, improve energy access, and promote sustainable development.

This initiative also highlights the importance of youth leadership in shaping the future of energy. By investing in young innovators, the program helps ensure that diverse perspectives and creative solutions are included in the transition to a low-carbon economy.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the accelerator will benefit from a tailored program specifically designed for renewable energy and climate technology startups. Through structured training and mentorship, they will gain practical insights into business development, market expansion, and investment readiness.

The program offers direct access to experienced mentors and industry experts who provide guidance on refining business strategies and overcoming operational challenges. This support helps participants strengthen their ventures and position them for long-term success.

In addition, startups will have opportunities to participate in pitching events and investment matchmaking sessions. These engagements allow participants to present their ideas to potential investors and partners, increasing their chances of securing funding and scaling their impact.

The accelerator also provides access to a global network of peers, professionals, and organizations working in the climate and energy sectors. This network can be a valuable resource for collaboration, knowledge sharing, and future partnerships.

Furthermore, selected participants may be considered for the IRENA Youth Award, which recognizes outstanding solutions and provides additional support to help scale successful projects.

Who Can Apply

The program is open to young entrepreneurs from around the world who are leading startups or growth-stage businesses in renewable energy, energy efficiency, or clean technology. Applicants must be between 18 and 35 years old, with at least one founder under the age of 35.

Eligible applicants should be actively involved in the development of their startup and committed to participating in the program. At least two founders must be able to dedicate approximately 16 hours per month to attend accelerator sessions and activities.

The startup’s main business focus should align with one of the following areas: renewable energy technologies such as solar, wind, hydropower, green hydrogen, geothermal, or bioenergy; climate adaptation technologies including water management, agriculture, and early warning systems; or climate mitigation technologies such as transportation, decarbonization, circular economy, and smart grids.

Eligible Areas

The accelerator is open globally, allowing startups from any country to apply.

Application Process and Deadline

Applications must be submitted online during the official application period. Interested applicants are encouraged to prepare their materials in advance, including their startup profile and supporting documentation.

The application window opens on 6 April 2026 and closes on 3 May 2026. Following the submission period, applications will be reviewed, and selected participants will be announced on 18 May 2026.

The accelerator program will run from 1 June to 30 August 2026, followed by business linkage opportunities and post-acceleration engagement in September.

Learn more and apply on the IRENA Website.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post IRENA NewGen Renewable Energy Accelerator 2026: Global Program to Scale Youth-Led Climate Startups appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: DIV Fund 2026: Get Funding to Scale Innovations That Impact Millions Globally
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Development Innovation Ventures (DIV) Fund has officially opened its Request for Proposals, inviting innovators, organizations, and changemakers to apply for funding that supports scalable, cost-effective solutions in global development. If you are working on an idea that can improve the lives of people living in poverty, this is a powerful opportunity to access funding […]

The post DIV Fund 2026: Get Funding to Scale Innovations That Impact Millions Globally appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Development Innovation Ventures (DIV) Fund has officially opened its Request for Proposals, inviting innovators, organizations, and changemakers to apply for funding that supports scalable, cost-effective solutions in global development.

If you are working on an idea that can improve the lives of people living in poverty, this is a powerful opportunity to access funding and scale your impact across sectors and regions worldwide.

What Is the DIV Fund?

The DIV Fund supports breakthrough innovations that address some of the world’s most pressing development challenges. The program focuses on solutions that are backed by evidence, cost-effective, and capable of reaching millions of people.

Unlike many traditional funding programs, DIV emphasizes measurable outcomes and scalability, ensuring that supported projects deliver real, lasting change.

What the Fund Supports

The DIV Fund provides funding across three key stages of innovation:

  • Pilots: Early-stage ideas that need testing and validation
  • Rigorous Evaluations: Projects with initial traction that require deeper evidence and impact measurement
  • Scale-Ups: Proven solutions ready to expand and reach millions

This tiered approach ensures that innovations receive the right level of support at each stage of their growth journey.

Key Focus Areas

The fund is open to innovations across all sectors and geographies, as long as they aim to improve outcomes for people living in poverty. These may include:

  • Health and public health solutions
  • Agriculture and food security
  • Education and skills development
  • Financial inclusion and economic empowerment
  • Climate resilience and sustainability

What Makes a Strong Application?

To stand out, applicants must demonstrate that their solution is:

  • Evidence-driven: Supported by data or a clear theory of change
  • Cost-effective: Able to deliver impact efficiently
  • Scalable: Designed to reach a large number of people

Projects that clearly show measurable outcomes and a pathway to scale will have a strong advantage.

Who Can Apply

The DIV Fund is open to a wide range of applicants, including:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Social enterprises and startups
  • Private sector companies
  • Research institutions
  • Individual innovators and partnerships

Application Timeline

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, meaning there is no fixed deadline. Early submission is highly recommended to increase your chances of selection.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and submit their proposals through the official application page.

For more Grant Opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post DIV Fund 2026: Get Funding to Scale Innovations That Impact Millions Globally appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026: A Fully Funded Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Immigrants in Arts, Science and Society (Up to $20,000 in Funding)
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
If your organization is committed to uplifting immigrant communities through arts, science, education, or social impact, the Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026 present a powerful funding opportunity. This prestigious grant program offers financial support to innovative nonprofit organizations working across the United States and its territories, helping them expand their reach and amplify their impact. About […]

The post Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026: A Fully Funded Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Immigrants in Arts, Science and Society (Up to $20,000 in Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
If your organization is committed to uplifting immigrant communities through arts, science, education, or social impact, the Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026 present a powerful funding opportunity. This prestigious grant program offers financial support to innovative nonprofit organizations working across the United States and its territories, helping them expand their reach and amplify their impact.

About the Vilcek Foundation Grants

The Vilcek Foundation is a U.S.-based nonprofit organization established in 2000 with a mission to recognize and celebrate immigrant contributions to the arts and sciences. Over the years, it has awarded millions of dollars in grants and prizes to individuals and organizations making a difference in these fields.

The Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026 continue this legacy by providing financial support to nonprofit organizations that promote diversity, innovation, and inclusion—especially those serving immigrant communities.

Grant Value and Funding Details

  • Total funding available: $200,000
  • Individual grant size: Up to $20,000
  • Typical funding range: $5,000 – $20,000
  • Project duration: Approximately 12 months
  • Grant period: September 1, 2026 – August 31, 2027

These grants are designed to support both project-based initiatives and organizational operations that align with the foundation’s mission.

Who Can Apply? (Eligible Categories of Applicants)

This opportunity is specifically targeted at:

1. Nonprofit Organizations

  • Must operate under IRS Section 501(c)(3)
  • Must be in good standing with tax-exempt status

2. Geographic Eligibility

  • Organizations must be based in:
    • The United States, or
    • U.S. Territories

3. Sector Focus

Eligible organizations must demonstrate work in at least one of the following areas:

  • Arts and culture
  • Science and research (non-laboratory/community-focused)
  • Education
  • Social services
  • Community engagement programs

4. Mission Alignment

Organizations should:

  • Support immigrant artists, scientists, or communities
  • Promote diversity, inclusion, and innovation
  • Deliver impactful and creative programs

Who Is NOT Eligible?

The following are generally excluded:

  • Individuals (this is not an individual grant)
  • Organizations outside the U.S. and its territories
  • Projects not focused on immigrant contributions
  • Certain research-only or K-12 institutional projects

Key Focus Areas of the Grant

The Vilcek Foundation prioritizes organizations that:

1. Empower Immigrant Communities – Programs that highlight immigrant contributions and improve access to opportunities.

2. Promote Arts and Culture – Support for exhibitions, performances, storytelling, and creative expression.

3. Advance Education and Knowledge Sharing – Initiatives that expand access to learning and community engagement.

4. Provide Social Services – Programs addressing real-world needs such as integration, employment, and wellbeing.

5. Demonstrate Innovation – Projects that stand out for their unique approach and measurable impact are highly favored.

Selection Criteria

Applications are evaluated based on:

  • Impact and relevance of the proposed project
  • Innovation and creativity in execution
  • Alignment with immigrant-focused mission
  • Organizational capacity and sustainability
  • Clarity and feasibility of the proposal

The foundation specifically looks for organizations that “set themselves apart” through unique and meaningful contributions.

Application Process

Applicants must:

  1. Prepare Organizational Information
    • Legal status documentation
    • Financial records
    • Organizational history
  2. Develop a Strong Proposal
    • Clear objectives
    • Defined target population
    • Measurable outcomes
  3. Submit via the Official Grant Portal
    • Online application system provided by the foundation
  4. Wait for Review and Notification
    • Applications reviewed after the deadline
    • Notifications expected around August–Q4 2026

Apply here

Why You Should Apply

The Vilcek Foundation Grants are more than just financial support—they offer:

  • Recognition from a globally respected foundation
  • Increased visibility for your organization
  • Opportunities to scale impactful programs
  • A chance to contribute to a broader narrative celebrating immigrants

Since its founding, the foundation has awarded millions of dollars to organizations across multiple states and territories, making it a highly credible and competitive funding source.

Important Tips for Applicants

  • Focus on immigrant-centered impact
  • Clearly demonstrate innovation and uniqueness
  • Provide strong evidence of outcomes and sustainability
  • Ensure all required documents are complete
  • Apply early to avoid submission issues

Deadline

30th April 2026

The Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026 represent a significant opportunity for nonprofit organizations committed to advancing immigrant contributions in society. With up to $20,000 in funding per organization and a strong emphasis on creativity and impact, this grant can be transformative for mission-driven organizations. If your organization operates within the United States and aligns with the foundation’s vision, this is a highly valuable opportunity worth pursuing.

For more information visit here

For more global opportunities click here

The post Vilcek Foundation Grants 2026: A Fully Funded Opportunity for Nonprofits Supporting Immigrants in Arts, Science and Society (Up to $20,000 in Funding) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
